---
title: "Jason_Code"
output: word_document
date: "2026-03-26"
---

```{r}

# ==============================================================================================
# MATH 4939 Project: Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI) Analysis
# Part 1: Structural Degeneration
# Part 2: Recovery Factors
# Part 3: Clinical Outcomes
# ==============================================================================================

# Load necessary libraries
library(readxl)
library(spida2)
library(lattice)
library(knitr)
library(nlme)
library(effects)
library(ggdag)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

load_file <- "C:/Users/Om Dhembare/Downloads/TBI.xlsx"
data_wide <- read_excel(load_file)

# Convert to long format using spida2
data_long <- tolong(data_wide, sep = "_")

# Ensure outcome and recovery variables are numeric (Using correct column name: LAQ_cogtot)
data_long$HPC_L_TOT <- as.numeric(data_long$HPC_L_TOT)
data_long$VBR <- as.numeric(data_long$VBR)
data_long$tothrsCOG <- as.numeric(data_long$tothrsCOG)
data_long$LAQ_cogtot <- as.numeric(data_long$LAQ_cogtot)

# --- INJURED DATA PROCESSING ---
data_patients <- subset(data_long, !grepl("^c", SubID) & time >= 2)
data_patients$date <- as.Date(data_patients$date)
data_patients$DOI  <- as.Date(data_patients$DOI)
data_patients$time_years <- as.numeric(difftime(data_patients$date, data_patients$DOI, units = "days")) / 365.25

data_patients <- subset(data_patients, !is.na(time_years) & !is.na(HPC_L_TOT))
data_patients <- subset(data_patients, SubID %in% names(which(table(SubID) >= 2)))
data_patients$Group <- "Injured"

# --- CONTROL DATA PROCESSING ---
data_controls <- subset(data_long, grepl("^c", SubID) & time >= 2)
data_controls <- merge(data_controls, aggregate(date ~ SubID, data = data_controls, min), by = "SubID", suffixes = c("", "_base"))
data_controls$date <- as.Date(data_controls$date)
data_controls$date_base <- as.Date(data_controls$date_base)
data_controls$time_years <- as.numeric(difftime(data_controls$date, data_controls$date_base, units = "days")) / 365.25

data_controls <- subset(data_controls, !is.na(time_years) & !is.na(HPC_L_TOT))
data_controls <- subset(data_controls, SubID %in% names(which(table(SubID) >= 2)))
data_controls$Group <- "Control"

# --- COMBINE INTO MASTER DATASET ---
keep_cols <- c("SubID", "time_years", "HPC_L_TOT", "Group", "age", "sex", "VBR", "tothrsCOG", "LAQ_cogtot")

data_master <- rbind(
  data_patients[, keep_cols],
  data_controls[, keep_cols]
)

data_master$Group <- as.factor(data_master$Group)
data_master$Group <- relevel(data_master$Group, ref = "Control")


# ==============================================================================================
# SECTION 1: BASELINE STRUCTURAL DEGENERATION 
# Question: Does TBI accelerate brain volume loss compared to normal aging?
# ==============================================================================================

# 1.1 Trajectory Plot
plot_comparison <- xyplot(HPC_L_TOT ~ time_years | Group,
                          data = data_master,
                          group = SubID,
                          type = c("p", "r"),
                          main = "Individual Trajectories: Healthy Aging vs. TBI Degeneration",
                          xlab = "Years Since Baseline/Injury",
                          ylab = "Left Hippocampus Volume (mm3)",
                          layout = c(2, 1),
                          grid = TRUE)
print(plot_comparison)

best_model <- lme(HPC_L_TOT ~ time_years * Group,
                  random = ~ 1 + time_years | SubID,
                  data = data_master,
                  control = lmeControl(opt = "optim"),
                  na.action = na.omit)

kable(round(summary(best_model)$tTable, 4), caption = "Base LMM Results")

# 1.2 Adjusted Linear Mixed Model (Controlling for Age & Sex)
adjusted_model <- lme(HPC_L_TOT ~ time_years * Group + age + sex,
                      random = ~ 1 + time_years | SubID,
                      data = data_master,
                      control = lmeControl(opt = "optim"),
                      na.action = na.omit)

kable(round(summary(adjusted_model)$tTable, 4), caption = "Adjusted LMM Results: Age & Sex Controlled")

# 1.3 10-Year Prediction Table
intercept_value <- summary(adjusted_model)$tTable["(Intercept)", "Value"]
injury_slope    <- summary(adjusted_model)$tTable["time_years:GroupInjured", "Value"]
baseline_slope  <- summary(adjusted_model)$tTable["time_years", "Value"]
total_patient_slope <- baseline_slope + injury_slope

years <- c(0, 2, 5, 10)
control_pred <- intercept_value + (baseline_slope * years)
injured_pred <- (intercept_value + summary(adjusted_model)$tTable["GroupInjured", "Value"]) + (total_patient_slope * years)

prediction_table <- data.frame(
  Year = years,
  Control_Vol = round(control_pred, 1),
  Injured_Vol = round(injured_pred, 1),
  Gap_mm3 = round(control_pred - injured_pred, 1)
)
prediction_table$Total_Pct_Loss <- round((prediction_table$Gap_mm3 / prediction_table$Control_Vol) * 100, 1)

kable(prediction_table, col.names = c("Year", "Healthy Vol", "Injured Vol", "Volume Gap", "% Total Loss"),
      caption = "The Progressive Toll of TBI: Cumulative Volume Loss")

# 1.4 DAG: Justifying Confounders
causal_dag <- dagify(
  HPC_Volume ~ TBI + Age + Sex,
  TBI ~ Age + Sex,
  exposure = "TBI",
  outcome = "HPC_Volume",
  labels = c("HPC_Volume" = "Left HPC Volume", "TBI" = "TBI (Group)", "Age" = "Age at Baseline", "Sex" = "Sex")
)

plot_dag <- ggdag(causal_dag, text = FALSE, use_labels = "label") + theme_dag() +
  ggplot2::labs(title = "Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG): Causal Pathways",
                subtitle = "Justifying Age and Sex as necessary confounders")
print(plot_dag)

# 1.5 Cross-Validation: The Vacuum Effect (HPC vs VBR)
vbr_model <- lme(VBR ~ time_years * Group + age + sex, random = ~ 1 + time_years | SubID, 
                 data = data_master, control = lmeControl(opt = "optim"), na.action = na.omit)

hpc_slopes <- ranef(adjusted_model)[, "time_years", drop = FALSE]
vbr_slopes <- ranef(vbr_model)[, "time_years", drop = FALSE]

slope_corr_df <- data.frame(SubID = rownames(hpc_slopes), HPC_Slope = hpc_slopes$time_years, VBR_Slope = vbr_slopes$time_years)

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
plot(slope_corr_df$HPC_Slope, slope_corr_df$VBR_Slope,
     main = "The Vacuum Effect: Tissue Loss vs. Ventricle Expansion",
     xlab = "HPC Slope (Negative = Shrinking)", ylab = "VBR Slope (Positive = Expanding)",
     pch = 19, col = "darkblue")
abline(lm(VBR_Slope ~ HPC_Slope, data = slope_corr_df), col = "red", lwd = 2)

## Interpretation: Significant negative correlation. As the hippocampus shrinks (tissue dies), 
## the ventricles expand (fill with fluid), validating our structural degeneration findings.


# ==============================================================================================
# SECTION 2: RECOVERY FACTORS (Cognitive Therapy)
# Question: Does cognitive therapy mitigate the shrinkage of the hippocampus?
# ==============================================================================================

# Subset injured patients
recovery_data <- subset(data_master, Group == "Injured")
recovery_data <- subset(recovery_data, !is.na(tothrsCOG))

therapy_model <- lme(HPC_L_TOT ~ time_years * tothrsCOG + age + sex,
                     random = ~ 1 + time_years | SubID,
                     data = recovery_data,
                     control = lmeControl(opt = "optim"),
                     na.action = na.omit)

kable(round(summary(therapy_model)$tTable, 4), caption = "Recovery LMM: Protective Trend of Cognitive Therapy")

## Interpretation: The interaction term 'time_years:tothrsCOG' is positive (+0.1057). 
## While not statistically significant (p = 0.95), the positive coefficient indicates a directional trend


# ==============================================================================================
# SECTION 3: Clinical Outcomes
# Question: Does retaining hippocampus volume guarantee a better cognitive quality of life?
# ==============================================================================================

# Filter out missing LAQ scores (Patients only)
plot_data <- na.omit(data_master[data_master$Group == "Injured", c("HPC_L_TOT", "LAQ_cogtot")])

# 3.1 Visualizing the Paradox
comparison_plot <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = HPC_L_TOT, y = LAQ_cogtot)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6, color = "pink") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, color = "darkred", linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(title = "The Structure-Function Paradox in TBI Patients",
       subtitle = "Null correlation (p=0.60): Larger brain volume does NOT dictate higher cognitive QoL",
       x = "Left Hippocampus Volume (mm3)",
       y = "Cognitive LAQ Score (Higher = Better Function)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14))

print(comparison_plot)

# 3.2 Statistical Validation 
functional_model <- lm(LAQ_cogtot ~ HPC_L_TOT, data = plot_data)

# Extract coefficients and round them
model_coef <- round(summary(functional_model)$coefficients, 4)

# Render the table with clean, custom column names
kable(model_coef, 
      col.names = c("Estimate", "Std.Error", "t-value", "p-value"),
      caption = "Linear Model: Predicting Cognitive Score from Brain Volume")


## Interpretation: patients with larger preserved hippocampal volumes did NOT consistently report better cognitive Quality of Life (p = 0.60).
## Patients with low volume are reported high QoL, and vice versa.


par(mfrow = c(1, 3))

# 1. Fitted vs. Residuals (Check for homoscedasticity)
plot(fitted(adjusted_model), resid(adjusted_model),
     main = "Fitted vs. Residuals",
     xlab = "Fitted Values", ylab = "Residuals",
     pch = 20, col = "darkgray")
abline(h = 0, col = "red", lwd = 2)

# 2. Q-Q Plot of Residuals (Check for normality of residuals)
qqnorm(resid(adjusted_model), main = "Q-Q Plot: Model Residuals")
qqline(resid(adjusted_model), col = "blue", lwd = 2)

# 3. Q-Q Plot of Random Effects (Check normality of random intercepts)
qqnorm(ranef(adjusted_model)[,1], main = "Q-Q Plot: Random Intercepts")
qqline(ranef(adjusted_model)[,1], col = "green", lwd = 2)


```
